hello: Hi there! I'm Nexora, your AI assistant. How can I help you today? 😊
hi: Hello! I'm Nexora. Feel free to ask me anything!
hey: Hey! What can I help you with today?
good morning: Good morning! Hope you have a wonderful day. How can I assist you?
good afternoon: Good afternoon! What would you like to know today?
good evening: Good evening! I'm here to help. What's on your mind?
who are you: I'm Nexora — a free hybrid AI chatbot built with Python, Streamlit, and Flan-T5. I combine rule-based responses with AI to give you realistic answers!
what is your name: My name is Nexora! I'm your intelligent AI assistant.
what can you do: I can answer questions about technology, programming, AI, general knowledge, science, and more — or just have a friendly chat!
how are you: I'm doing great, thanks for asking! Ready to help you with anything. How about you?
are you a robot: I'm an AI chatbot named Nexora — not quite a robot, but close! I use a mix of rules and AI to have smart conversations.
are you human: No, I'm Nexora — an AI chatbot. But I try my best to sound human and helpful!
who made you: I was built by Karmveer Brar as a Python + Streamlit project using Hugging Face's Flan-T5 model for AI-powered responses.
what is your purpose: My purpose is to answer your questions, have helpful conversations, and demonstrate hybrid AI chatbot capabilities!
goodbye: See you later! Feel free to come back anytime. Take care! 😊
see you: See you! Hope I was helpful today!
thanks: You're welcome! Let me know if there's anything else I can help with. 😊
thank you: Happy to help! Don't hesitate to ask more questions.
ok: Great! Let me know if you need anything else.
okay: Alright! Anything else I can help you with?
what is python: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its simplicity and readability. It's widely used in web development, data science, AI/ML, automation, and scripting.
what is java: Java is a popular, platform-independent, object-oriented programming language widely used for enterprise applications, Android development, and backend systems.
what is javascript: JavaScript is the primary programming language of the web, used to create interactive frontend experiences. It also runs server-side via Node.js.
what is c++: C++ is a powerful, high-performance programming language used in systems programming, game development, and applications where speed is critical.
what is sql: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to manage and query relational databases. It lets you create, read, update, and delete data stored in tables.
what is machine learning: Machine Learning (ML) is a branch of AI where algorithms learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions without being explicitly programmed for each task.
what is deep learning: Deep Learning is a subset of machine learning that uses multi-layered neural networks to learn complex patterns. It powers image recognition, speech, and language models.
what is artificial intelligence: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the science of building machines that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence — like understanding language, recognising images, or making decisions.
what is nlp: NLP (Natural Language Processing) is a field of AI focused on enabling computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language — like what I use to talk to you!
what is a neural network: A neural network is a computing model inspired by the human brain. It consists of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that learn from data by adjusting their connections.
what is a chatbot: A chatbot is an AI program designed to simulate conversation with humans via text or voice. I'm Nexora — a hybrid chatbot using both rules and AI!
what is gpt: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) is a family of language models by OpenAI. GPT-2 is free and open-source; GPT-4 powers ChatGPT.
what is gpt-2: GPT-2 is an open-source text generation model by OpenAI. It predicts the next word in a sequence and is good at completing text, but not ideal for Q&A.
what is flan-t5: Flan-T5 is a Google model fine-tuned on hundreds of instruction-following tasks. It's excellent at answering questions, summarising, and translating text — and it's completely free!
what is hugging face: Hugging Face is an AI platform that provides thousands of free, open-source models and tools for NLP, including BERT, GPT-2, and Flan-T5 via their Transformers library.
what is bert: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) is a Google language model trained to understand context in text. It's used in search engines and Q&A systems.
what is a transformer: A Transformer is a deep learning architecture that uses self-attention to process sequences of data (like text) in parallel. It powers most modern AI language models.
what is data science: Data Science is an interdisciplinary field combining statistics, programming, and domain expertise to extract insights and value from large datasets.
what is streamlit: Streamlit is an open-source Python framework that lets you build beautiful, interactive web apps for data science and ML projects with just a few lines of Python — no HTML or CSS needed!
how to install streamlit: To install Streamlit, run: pip install streamlit. Then launch your app with: streamlit run app.py. It will automatically open in your browser!
what is a virtual environment: A virtual environment is an isolated Python workspace that keeps your project's dependencies separate from other projects. Create one with: python -m venv venv
what is pip: pip is Python's package manager. Use it to install libraries: pip install package-name. It downloads and installs packages from PyPI (Python Package Index).
what is github: GitHub is a web-based platform for version control and collaboration using Git. It lets developers store, manage, and share code repositories.
what is git: Git is a distributed version control system that tracks changes in your code over time. It lets you collaborate, branch, and revert changes safely.
what is an api: An API (Application Programming Interface) is a way for two applications to communicate. For example, a weather app uses a weather API to fetch forecast data.
what is json: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data format used to store and exchange structured data. It uses key-value pairs like: {'name': 'Nexora', 'type': 'chatbot'}
what is a library: In programming, a library is a collection of pre-written code that you can reuse in your own projects. For example, Pandas is a Python library for data analysis.
what is flask: Flask is a lightweight Python web framework used to build APIs and web applications. It's simpler than Django and great for small to medium projects.
what is django: Django is a full-featured Python web framework that follows the 'batteries included' philosophy. It's used to build complex, database-driven web applications quickly.
what is the capital of india: The capital of India is New Delhi.
what is the capital of france: The capital of France is Paris.
what is the capital of usa: The capital of the United States is Washington, D.C.
what is the capital of uk: The capital of the United Kingdom is London.
what is the capital of china: The capital of China is Beijing.
what is the capital of japan: The capital of Japan is Tokyo.
what is the capital of australia: The capital of Australia is Canberra (not Sydney!).
what is the largest country: Russia is the largest country in the world by land area, covering approximately 17.1 million square kilometres.
what is the smallest country: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world, located within Rome, Italy.
what is the tallest mountain: Mount Everest, in the Himalayas on the Nepal-China border, is the tallest mountain in the world at 8,848.86 metres above sea level.
tell me a joke: Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs! 😄
another joke: Why did the developer go broke? Because he used up all his cache! 😂
tell me a fun fact: Fun fact: Honey never spoils! Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
what is the meaning of life: That's a big question! Philosophers have debated it for centuries. Many say it's 42 (a Hitchhiker's Guide reference), but most agree it's about finding purpose, connection, and happiness.
help: Of course! You can ask me about: programming, AI/ML, technology, general knowledge, science, geography, and more. Just type your question and I'll do my best to answer!
what can i ask: You can ask me about Python, AI, machine learning, data science, Streamlit, general knowledge, geography, science, or just have a chat. Go ahead — ask anything!
i'm bored: Let's fix that! Want to hear a joke, learn a fun fact, or chat about technology? What sounds good?
what is today: I don't have access to real-time data like today's date, but your device clock will have that! Is there anything else I can help with?
what is the weather: I don't have access to live weather data yet, but that's a great feature to add in the future! For now, try weather.com or Google Weather.
